Infrastructure as Code Patterns

Master Infrastructure as Code with Cursor and Claude Code. This guide covers Terraform modules, CloudFormation templates, Pulumi programs, Ansible playbooks, GitOps workflows, and production IaC patterns with AI assistance.

  1. Initialize IaC Project

    • Cursor
    • Claude Code

  2. Install IaC MCP Servers (optional)

  3. Configure AI Rules

Best Practices

Section titled “Best Practices”

Infrastructure as Code Guidelines

1. Version Control Everything

Section titled “1. Version Control Everything”
  • Store all infrastructure code in Git
  • Use meaningful commit messages
  • Tag releases for production deployments
  • Implement branch protection rules
  • Review all changes through pull requests

2. Modular Design

Section titled “2. Modular Design”
  • Create reusable modules
  • Keep modules focused and single-purpose
  • Version your modules
  • Document module interfaces
  • Test modules independently

3. State Management

Section titled “3. State Management”
  • Use remote state backends
  • Enable state locking
  • Implement state file encryption
  • Regular state backups
  • Separate states by environment

4. Security First

Section titled “4. Security First”
  • Never commit secrets
  • Use secret management tools
  • Implement least privilege
  • Enable audit logging
  • Regular security scanning

5. Testing Strategy

Section titled “5. Testing Strategy”
  • Unit test modules
  • Integration test deployments
  • Compliance validation
  • Cost estimation
  • Performance testing

6. Documentation

Section titled “6. Documentation”
  • Document architecture decisions
  • Maintain runbooks
  • Create deployment guides
  • Document troubleshooting steps
  • Keep examples updated
